items.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq Post Weekly Losses as Tech Sell-Off Continues
items.content: US stocks fell on Friday June 26, with S&P 500 and Nasdaq posting weekly losses as tech sell-off extended. Chip stocks led declines with Intel -3%, Sandisk -10%, Arm -4%, Marvell -5%.

items.title: Global tech stocks sell off on AI infrastructure cost concerns
items.content: Chip stocks sold off globally over rising AI infrastructure costs. SMIC tumbled nearly 7%. Magnificent Seven all down at least 8% in June. OpenAI IPO may delay to 2027.

items.title: May PCE inflation hits 4.1%, highest since April 2023
items.content: Fed's preferred inflation gauge hit 4.1% annual rate in May, highest since April 2023. Core PCE at 3.4%, highest since Oct 2023. Energy prices driven by Iran conflict remain the main driver. Fed rate hike in September still on the table.

items.title: May US PCE inflation tops 4%, leaves Fed hike on the table
items.content: Personal consumption expenditures rose 0.7% monthly, above forecast. Personal income also climbed. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh committed to bringing inflation down.

items.title: Personal Income and Outlays, May 2026
items.content: Core PCE (ex food & energy) increased 3.4% year-over-year. Monthly PCE price index rose 0.5% MoM in May, accelerating from 0.4% in April.

items.title: Hang Seng Index Drops 1.76% to 22,671 as AI Sell-Off Hits One-Year Low
items.content: Hong Kong Hang Seng Index closed Friday June 26 at 22,671, down 1.76%, hitting a one-year low. AI-related sell-off weighed heavily on the market.

items.title: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Rebound From Edge of Bear Market
items.content: Hang Seng China Enterprises Index narrowed decline to 0.3% from as much as 2.3%. AI-linked names driving volatility. China Life Insurance among insurers stabilizing the market.

items.title: SSE Composite Slides 2.26% to 4,027 as Global AI Sell-Off Hits China Markets
items.content: China's Shanghai Composite fell 2.26% to 4,027 on June 26. Global AI sell-off and OpenAI IPO delay rattled China markets. Year-over-year gain still ~21%.

items.title: Iran closed Strait of Hormuz on June 20; US conducts freedom of navigation ops
items.content: Iran declared closure of Strait of Hormuz on June 20. US conducted freedom of navigation operations - first naval presence in the Strait since war began. Ceasefire terms remain fragile.

items.title: US-Iran ceasefire implementation remains tense
items.content: Trump announced renewed Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire on June 19. Iran closed Strait of Hormuz on June 20. US ships entered Strait on freedom of navigation mission. Broader peace agreement MOU near finalization.

items.title: Micron tops Q3 earnings estimates with better-than-expected outlook
items.content: Micron (MU) beat Q3 earnings estimates on top and bottom lines, declared dividend. However stock fell 6.69% following the announcement as broader AI sell-off weighed.

items.title: Dow Jones hits record close of 51,999.67
items.content: Dow Jones Industrial Average rose to record close of 51,999.67 on June 16, up 0.64%, as investors rotated out of chipmakers into cyclical stocks amid declining oil prices.
